clippy::never_loop: Refactor this loop to perform more than one iteration or remove it.
clippy::iter_next_loop: Remove or replace the loop over the result of this `next()` call.
clippy::impl_hash_borrow_with_str_and_by: Remove one of the incompatible `Hash`, `Borrow<str>` or `Borrow<[u8]>` implementations.
clippy::match_str_case_mismatch: Refactor this `match` so that all arms compare strings in consistent casing.
clippy::cast_slice_different_sizes: Replace this potentially incorrect cast with a safe alternative.
clippy::lint_groups_priority: Use `priority` field in the `[lints]` table in `Cargo.toml` to override a group of lints. The priority field ensures the correct order of linting.
clippy::self_assignment: Remove or correct this useless self-assignment.
clippy::modulo_one: Refactor the code to remove the `% 1` or `% -1` operation.
clippy::erasing_op: Replace `x * 0`, `0 / x`, or `x & 0` with a direct constant value.
clippy::eq_op: Correct expressions where identical variables are used on both sides of a binary operator.
clippy::impossible_comparisons: Refactor or remove this comparison that always evaluates to false.
clippy::ineffective_bit_mask: Remove this unnecessary bit mask operation.
clippy::bad_bit_mask: Correct this bit mask to ensure the comparison does not always yield the same result.
clippy::absurd_extreme_comparisons: Remove the unnecessary comparison or correct the logic to use an appropriate check.
clippy::deprecated_semver: Use a valid semantic version for the 'since' field, or "TBD" for future deprecation.
clippy::iter_skip_zero: Remove `.skip(0)` from the iterator or change it to `.skip(1)` if the intention is to skip the first element.
clippy::vec_resize_to_zero: Replace `Vec::resize(0, value)` with `Vec::clear()`, or swap the `Vec::resize` arguments.
clippy::iterator_step_by_zero: Correct the step value in this `.step_by(0)` call.
clippy::mistyped_literal_suffixes: Use the correct suffix for this literal to represent the intended value.
clippy::approx_constant: Replace approximate floating-point literals with their corresponding predefined constants from the standard library.
clippy::min_max: Correct the order of min and max functions to avoid unintended constant results.
clippy::let_underscore_lock: Refactor this code to not drop the lock guard immediately after acquiring it.
clippy::redundant_locals: Remove redundant redefinitions of local variables to improve code clarity and avoid potential issues.
clippy::ifs_same_cond: Eliminate or alter consecutive `if` statements that share the same condition to prevent potential copy-paste errors.
clippy::infinite_iter: Apply a terminating operation to this infinite iterator.
clippy::invalid_null_ptr_usage: Use `NonNull::dangling().as_ptr()` instead of `ptr::null()` to prevent undefined behavior.
clippy::inline_fn_without_body: Remove the `#[inline]` attribute from this trait method; it has no effect.
clippy::invalid_regex: Correct the regex pattern to ensure it compiles without errors.
clippy::out_of_bounds_indexing: Accessing an array with an out-of-bounds index will cause a runtime panic.
clippy::invisible_characters: Remove this invisible character from this literal.
clippy::async_yields_async: Add `.await` to the returned future within the async context.
clippy::mem_replace_with_uninit: Replace this value with a safer alternative to avoid undefined behavior.
clippy::overly_complex_bool_expr: Simplify this boolean expression and eliminate unnecessary terminals.
clippy::enum_clike_unportable_variant: Replace this `repr(isize/usize)` with a portable type.
clippy::almost_swapped: Use `std::mem::swap(&mut var1, &mut var2)` instead of `var1 = var2; var2 = var1;`.
clippy::if_let_mutex: Refactor the code to ensure `Mutex::lock` is not called within an `if let` and also in the `else` block to avoid holding the lock for the entire `if let ... else` block.
clippy::possible_missing_comma: Ensure that array elements are separated by commas, especially when they contain binary operator expressions.
clippy::inherent_to_string_shadow_displa: Remove this `to_string` function as it will shadow the `Display` trait implementation.
clippy::eager_transmute: Make sure that this call to `std::mem::transmute` is lazily evaluated.
clippy::derive_ord_xor_partial_ord: Replace manual `PartialOrd` and `Ord` implementations with either entirely derived or entirely manual implementations.
clippy::derived_hash_with_manual_eq: Replace this manually implemented `PartialEq` with the derived implementation.
clippy::no_effect: Remove or correct this statement, as it has no effect.
clippy::too_many_arguments: Refactor the function to reduce the number of parameters.
clippy::zero_ptr: Use `ptr::null()` instead of `0 as` to cast to a null pointer.
clippy::redundant_closure_for_method_cal: Replace this closure with a method reference.
clippy::string_to_string: Remove the redundant `to_string()` call, or clone the string.
clippy::redundant_field_names: Use the field init shorthand syntax.
clippy::let_and_return: Remove the `let` statement and return the expression directly.
clippy::match_bool: Replace this `match` expression with an `if-else` expression.
clippy::misnamed_getter: Access the expected field of the getter.
clippy::print_stdout: Use a logging framework to log this message to the console.
clippy::while_float: Avoid using floating-point numbers as loop counters.
clippy::vec_init_then_push: Use a vector literal to initialize the vector.
clippy::unreadable_literal: Use underscore delimiters to make the literal easier to read.
clippy::wildcard_imports: Replace this wildcard import with specific imports.
clippy::branches_sharing_code: Refactor the code to avoid duplicating logic in multiple branches.
clippy::else_if_without_else: Add an `else` block to this `if`-`else if` chain.
clippy::zst_offset: Remove the use of `offset` or `wrapping_add`/`wrapping_sub` on pointers to zero-sized types.
clippy::wrong_transmute: Remove or correct this transmute operation.
clippy::while_immutable_condition: Ensure that variables within the while condition are mutated in the body of the loop.
clippy::useless_attribute: Remove this useless lint attribute, or place it on the imported items themselves.
clippy::unused_io_amount: Process the entire buffer with `write_all(&buffer)` or `read_exact(&buffer)`.
clippy::unsound_collection_transmute: Do not transmute collections of different types; use iteration and mapping instead.
clippy::unit_return_expecting_ord: Ensure the closure properly returns an `Ord` type or remove any extraneous semi-colons.
clippy::unit_hash: Replace `().hash(_)` with `0_u8.hash(_)` to yield a meaningful hash value.
clippy::unit_cmp: Remove comparisons and asserts involving the unit type `()`.
clippy::uninit_assumed_init: Prevent undefined behavior by not using `MaybeUninit::uninit().assume_init()`.
clippy::transmuting_null: Use `Option` to represent a nullable reference
clippy::transmute_null_to_fn: Use `Option<fn()>` to safely represent a nullable function pointer.
clippy::suspicious_splitn: Use `splitn` with at least two splits.
clippy::size_of_in_element_count: Use the element count directly instead of `size_of::<T>`.
clippy::reversed_empty_ranges: Invert the start and end values of the range, and reverse it with `.rev()`.
clippy::redundant_comparisons: Remove this redundant comparison, or correct the intended comparison logic.
clippy::recursive_format_impl: Refactor this formatting implementation to avoid infinite recursion.
clippy::read_line_without_trim: Always trim the trailing newline when using `Stdin::read_line`.
clippy::panicking_unwrap: Avoid using `unwrap()` in conditions that will always cause a panic. Use explicit `panic!()` if panicking is intended.
clippy::panicking_overflow_checks: Use safe methods like `checked_add` or `overflowing_add` instead of C-style overflow checks.
clippy::option_env_unwrap: Use `env!(...)` to ensure compile-time checking of the environment variable.
clippy::not_unsafe_ptr_arg_deref: Mark this function as `unsafe` to indicate that it dereferences raw pointer arguments.
clippy::nonsensical_open_options: Ensure that these file open options are consistent and do not conflict with each other.
clippy::non_octal_unix_permissions: Use octal values to set Unix file permissions.
clippy::uninit_vec: Initialize this vector before using it.
